I just went with common sense of what most people use when creating venv, the ideal thing would be to recommend a venv name and add that to the gitignore although adding venv is still not a bad idea.

> > most people use venv
> 
> Do you have a reference for that? We don't specify the name of the venv in our [dev setup instructions](https://matplotlib.org/devdocs/devel/development_setup.html#create-a-dedicated-environment). Numpy uses `numpy-dev` (https://numpy.org/doc/stable/dev/development_environment.html#using-virtual-environments). I feel a name containing the library is better because your prompt shows what environment you are in and if everything is named `venv` that's not too helpful.

I chose 'venv' based on the common practice among developers when creating virtual environments. However, I agree that recommending a more descriptive virtual environment name would be ideal for clarity. This checks if n is an interger
The problem arises because n can still be a valid integer (e.g., 0 or -1) that is not appropriate for the rfftfreq and fftfreq functions. The functions expect n to be a positive integer because:

Zero (n=0): The Fourier transform requires a non-zero window length to calculate frequency bins. A window length of zero would not make sense mathematically for the FFT, as you can’t compute frequencies from zero data points.

Negative (n<0): A negative window length doesn't make sense in the context of Fourier transforms. It would result in invalid or nonsensical calculations.

    if not isinstance(n, integer_types) or n <= 0:
        raise ValueError("n should be a positive integer")
